aboutme.aboutme-third-title: R&D engineer in embedded systems, specialising in security, advanced sensing and edge architectures.
aboutme.aboutme-first-paragraph: Electronics, robotics and mechatronics engineer with hands-on R&D experience spanning embedded security evaluation, advanced sensor integration and edge computing. I have worked on IoT certification (SESIP), the design of aeronautical platforms with NXP S32K344 and Jetson Orin NX, and the development of self-configurable wireless sensor network infrastructures for edge computing.
aboutme.aboutme-profile-text: Methodical and results-driven, with strong adaptability in technically demanding environments. I value good engineering practices — clear documentation, version control and reproducible evidence — and I build tools and scripts to streamline workflows, reduce errors and accelerate validation cycles.
aboutme.aboutme-interest-text: Embedded securityFirmware & reverse engineeringApplied cryptographyAutomation (Python/C/C++)IoT & networkingLinux & scriptingPCB designHiking

items.name: Cults3D — 3D Designs (CATIA V5 / scanning)
items.desc: Collection of 3D-printable models: original designs made in CATIA V5, 3D-scanned parts, and custom creations ready to download/print.

items.name: DGT — Traffic incidents & V16 beacons
items.desc: Traffic incidents map and V16 connected beacon context (DGT 3.0): HTML/JS frontend plus a Go proxy to consume data without CORS issues. Deployed on Cloudflare Pages and reproducible with Docker.

items.name: Face Recognition App
items.desc: Python face recognition app using computer vision libraries.

items.name: SDK Difference Scanner
items.desc: Python tool to scan and compare SDK differences (analysis and reporting).

items.name: ESP-NOW Gateway (Master thesis)
items.desc: ESP32 gateway with automatic connection based on ESP-NOW for IoT scenarios.

items.name: ESP-NOW Infrastructure (paper)
items.desc: Gateway extension with a custom data infrastructure to share data at the edge.

education.edu1-description: Official certification focused on mechanical CAD and part modeling.
education.edu2-description: Advanced training in mechatronic systems: electronics, control, software and system design.
education.edu3-title: Project Management for Solar PV Installations
education.edu3-description: PV project planning and management: regulations, risks, applications and impact.
education.edu4-title: B.Eng. in Electronics Engineering, Robotics and Mechatronics
education.edu4-description: Strong foundation in electronics, robotics and automation for embedded systems.
education.edu5-description: Full‑stack web programming and core web development fundamentals.
education.edu6-description: Training and qualification for drone operations (RPAS) and radio communications.

experience.exp0-description:
• First engineer to join the electronics department: technical definition and pre-design of an airside mobile platform for pilot assistance, obstacle detection and relative positioning.
• Hierarchical embedded architecture with NXP S32K344 as the control core and Jetson Orin NX as companion ECU for advanced perception and sensor pre-processing.
• Definition of the sensor chain (LiDAR Ouster OS2, radar Acconeer A121/A212) and electronic/communication integration under aeronautical normative frameworks DO-178 and DO-278.
experience.exp1-description:
• Security evaluation and certification (e.g., SESIP), including cryptographic analysis and design review.
• Validation of critical security functions: secure boot, secure storage, secure debug and firmware update flows.
• Testing IoT and networking technologies (BLE/LTE/Ethernet/MACsec), assessing pairing, encryption and key management.
• Test automation and evidence collection with Python and C/C++ scripts in Linux/VM environments.
experience.exp2-place: Computer Architecture Dept. — University of Málaga (UMA)
experience.exp2-description:
• Built IoT infrastructure and self-configurable sensor networks (ESP32, FreeRTOS) for edge computing.
• Data integration and automation using MQTT and Node-RED on UNIX servers.
• Technical support, co-supervision of BSc projects, and research contributions (RISC-V / sensing systems).
experience.exp2b-description:
• Developed and contributed to the drone design for the final project concept.
• 3D modeling and support in the mechanical definition of the aerial system.
• Role carried out in parallel with my main position at DEKRA as a remote part-time collaboration.
experience.exp3-description:
• Consulting and network deployment studies (mobile networks and FTTH).
• Reviewed engineering documentation and as-built files for school/public-site installations.

drone.title: Drone / Averon
drone.subtitle: 3D MODEL & CONTRIBUTION
drone.summary: During my part-time collaboration with Averon, I worked on the design and modeling of drones supporting the final concept of the project. The drone shown here was designed and modeled in CATIA V5, and is presented as an interactive 3D model.

hardware.title: Electronic Design
hardware.subtitle: PCB PROJECTS
hardware.dea-desc: 4-layer PCB designed with Eagle for the Advanced Electronic Design course of the M.Sc. in Mechatronics. The circuit implements a motor controller based on the Texas Instruments MSP430G2XX1 microcontroller, with power management distributed across independent layers to minimise interference between the signal and power stages. It includes overcurrent protection and an optimised layout to reduce EMI.
hardware.dea-3d: Interactive 3D model of the DEA PCB
hardware.consola-desc: 2-layer PCB designed with Altium Designer. A compact handheld console based on the ESP32 module, featuring integrated WiFi and Bluetooth connectivity. The design focuses on layout compactness and proper management of power rails, antenna routing and digital signals within a small form factor.
hardware.consola-3d: Interactive 3D model of the ESP32 Console

teaching.title: Teaching
teaching.subtitle: PRACTICUM & EDUCATIONAL RESOURCES
teaching.rea-kicker: OPEN EDUCATIONAL RESOURCE · MASTER IN EDUCATION
teaching.rea-desc: Complete OER built with ExeLearning for the Learning and Teaching of Technology subject. Includes IoT theoretical foundations, hands-on activities with ESP8266 and BMP280, assessment rubrics and multimedia resources aimed at secondary and sixth-form students.
teaching.p4eso-desc: IoT introduction project: students flash MicroPython onto a Wemos D1 mini, read temperature and pressure from a BMP280 sensor over I2C, and publish the data to an MQTT broker in JSON format. Includes firmware, library and copy-paste-ready commands for Windows.
teaching.p1bach-title: Advanced IoT Node with LWT & auto-reconnect
teaching.p1bach-desc: Advanced version of the IoT node programmed in C++ with the Arduino framework. Implements automatic MQTT broker reconnection, Online/Offline status via LWT (Last Will & Testament) and periodic JSON data publishing. Aimed at sixth-form students with programming experience.
teaching.pnotebooks-desc: Collection of Python exercises in Jupyter Notebook format, organised by teaching units with progressive learning. Each folder includes 10 activities + a challenge and a solutions notebook, ready to use on Google Colab or locally.
teaching.aulavista-desc: Master's Final Project in Education. A Python desktop application (PySide6) to record student observations, track their progress throughout the school year and support data-driven pedagogical decisions. Designed for real classroom use by secondary school teachers.
